items.title: Meta Launches New Facebook Marketplace App Called Seller
items.author: Eli Tan
items.content: <article>Seller is part of the Silicon Valley company’s strategy to innovate on its original social network.</article>

items.title: OpenAI Models Go Rogue + Kimi K3 Freakout + A.I. Superforecasting
items.author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Davis Land, John Woo, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano and Leah Shaw Dameron
items.content: <article>“The story that we’re talking about in this segment was science fiction until Tuesday, OK?”</article>

items.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
items.author: Ben Sisario
items.content: <article>Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.</article>

items.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
items.author: David McCabe
items.content: <article>The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.</article>

items.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
items.author: Don Clark
items.content: <article>The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.</article>

items.title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
items.author: Adam Satariano and Jeanna Smialek
items.content: <article>At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.</article>

items.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
items.author: Steve Lohr
items.content: <article>China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.</article>

items.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
items.author: Ivan Penn
items.content: <article>The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.</article>

items.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
items.author: Eli Tan and Cecilia Kang
items.content: <article>For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found liable in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.</article>

items.title: Ford Will Use Apple Maps in New Self-Driving System
items.author: Jack Ewing
items.content: <article>In a first, Apple software will influence the way a new line of electric vehicles made by Ford Motor operates, and other automakers may follow.</article>

items.title: Tesla Profit Falls Even as Car Sales Rebound
items.author: Jack Ewing
items.content: <article>The company is selling more cars, but the company’s profit was down because of price cuts and higher expenses.</article>

items.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
items.author: Brian X. Chen
items.content: <article>It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.</article>

items.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
items.author: David Yaffe-Bellany
items.content: <article>Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.</article>

items.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
items.author: Kate Conger
items.content: <article>Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.</article>

items.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
items.author: Stuart A. Thompson and Tiffany Hsu
items.content: <article>Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.</article>

items.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
items.author: Kate Conger
items.content: <article>The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.</article>

items.title: What Happened When Meta Used A.I. to Ban Accounts on Facebook and Instagram
items.author: Stuart A. Thompson and Eli Tan
items.content: <article>Users said the technology mistakenly deleted their accounts. To resolve the issue, they still had to rely on A.I.</article>

items.title: Google Releases Three New Gemini A.I. Models
items.author: Kate Conger
items.content: <article>The models include one that is the company’s most powerful and another that is fine-tuned for cybersecurity, as Google competes with rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic.</article>

items.title: When Listening to Music Was a Team Sport
items.author: Walker Mimms
items.content: <article>An exhibition at the Cooper Hewitt celebrates the glorious machines that gave us the soundtracks of our lives before streaming sites shoved them toward obsolescence.</article>

items.title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
items.author: Kate Conger
items.content: <article>As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.</article>

items.title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
items.author: David McCabe
items.content: <article>The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.</article>

items.title: A.I. ‘Vibecoded’ Apps Are Flooding Apple’s App Store
items.author: Kalley Huang
items.content: <article>Mobile apps are easy to make with artificial intelligence. That doesn’t mean people are excited to use them.</article>

items.title: Why Silicon Valley Can’t Stop Looking Over Its Shoulder at China
items.author: Cade Metz
items.content: <article>Chinese companies are offering artificial intelligence that is nearly as good as the leading U.S. technologies, but it’s still America’s race to lose.</article>

items.title: The ‘Bad Blood’ Between Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi’s Tarek Mansour
items.author: David Yaffe-Bellany and William K. Rashbaum
items.content: <article>Shayne Coplan of Polymarket and Tarek Mansour of Kalshi are young billionaires intent on dominating prediction markets. It’s more than a battle. It’s “bad blood.”</article>

items.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
items.author: Victor Mather
items.content: <article>Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.</article>

items.title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
items.author: Michael S. Rosenwald
items.content: <article>A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.</article>

items.title: Meta in Talks to Lease Computing Power to Anthropic in Potential $10 Billion Deal
items.author: Eli Tan and Mike Isaac
items.content: <article>A deal would underline how scarce computing power is for artificial intelligence development, and could create a new business for Meta.</article>
